'群馬県佐波郡玉村町下之宮', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 火雷神社 |画像 = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}大字下之宮甲524番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=火雷神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|火雷大神|Q10892534|火雷神}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
{{jalink|上野国|Q1054838}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = （伝）第10代{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}元年 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 下之宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月3日|Q2509}} |神事 = 麦蒔御神事（旧暦10月末-11月初） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=火雷神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''火雷神社'''（からいじんじゃ/ほのいかづちじんじゃ「からい」：群馬県神社庁、「ほのいかづち」：『群馬県の地名』。）は、{{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}、{{jalink|上野国|Q1054838}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|利根川|Q177452}}右岸に鎮座しており、左岸に鎮座する{{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}}（上野国九宮、上之宮）に対して「'''下之宮'''（しものみや）」と称される。 === 祭神 === '''主祭神''' * '''{{jalink|火雷大神|Q10892534|火雷神}}''' （ほのいかづちのかみ）祭神の記載は、拝殿横の説明板による。 '''配祀神''' * {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323|保食命}}、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真命}}、那波八郎命 * {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}}、{{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主命}}、{{jalink|建御名方神||健御名方命}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞鳴尊}}、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高淤賀美命}} * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|大日孁貴命}}、{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝では、第10代{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}元年の創建という『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』pp. 282-283。。その後{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}（第12代）の代に東国に派遣された{{jalink|御諸別王|Q17230461}}（崇神天皇四世孫）も当社を祭祀したと伝える。当社の奉斎氏族は、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}で功を成した佐味氏（佐味君）と考えられている。 鎮座地の地名「下之宮」は、当社に由来するとされており、利根川を挟んで「上之宮」の{{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}}と対峙するが、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}の利根川変流まで両社は地続きであったという。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は、『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）に{{jalink|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|Q11634952|賀茂神社}}・{{jalink|美和神社 (桐生市)|Q11608848|美和神社}}とともに「{{jalink|那波郡|Q11642759}}火雷神」を官社と為すという記事である。これら3神は、いずれも大和地方からの勧請と見られている（賀茂 = {{jalink|高鴨神社|Q11673295}}、美和 = {{jalink|大神神社|Q705542}}、火雷 = {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}}）。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では上野国那波郡に「火雷神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。『日本後紀』『延喜式』いずれにも「那波郡」と記載されるが、この郡は{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}（明治に{{jalink|佐位郡|Q11382916}}・那波郡を合併）の前身にあたる。 {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）の『上野国交替実録帳』には「正二位火雷明神社」と記されるとともに「神館壱宇、鳥居壱基、間垣壱廻」と記載がある。また『上野国神名帳』では、総社本では鎮守10社の8番目、一宮本では鎮守12社の10番目に「従一位火雷大明神」と記載されている。 {{jalink|南北朝時代 (日本)|Q264012|南北朝時代}}成立の『{{jalink|神道集|Q2616931}}』では「八宮ヲハ那波ノ上ノ宮、火雷神ト申ス」と記載があることから、当社は上野国の{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}であったと見られている（「上ノ宮」は誤記と見られる）。また同集によると、当社の{{jalink|本地仏}}は{{jalink|虚空蔵菩薩|Q416174}}であった。中世には{{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}}・{{jalink|新田氏|Q2274150}}から崇敬され、盛時には4町四方の社域を有したが、那波氏衰退とともに当社も零落したと伝える。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}により「火雷天神社」と称された。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）7月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168}} ** {{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）8月16日、官社に列す （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』）『日本後紀』延暦15年（796年）8月16日条。 * 六国史以後 ** {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）、正二位 （『上野国交替実録帳』）『群馬県の地名』火雷神社項。 - 表記は「火雷明神社」。 ** 従一位 （『上野国神名帳』） - 表記は「火雷大明神」。 === 境内 === 本殿は三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}で、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}2年（{{jalink|1645年|Q6925}}）に造営され、以後修復を繰り返した。 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|石祠群 === 祭事 === * 例祭 （4月3日） * 小祭 （10月17日） 上記のほか、旧暦10月末の午の日午前0時から旧暦11月初午の日の夜明けにかけての13日間、特殊神事「'''麦蒔御神事'''（むぎまきごしんじ）」が行われる。神事は貞観4年（{{jalink|862年|Q24993}}）に始まったといわれる。旧暦10月末の午の日の丑刻に秘密神事を行い、燈火を用いず微声で祝詞を奏上する境内説明板。。その後は旧暦11月初午の日まで、神社四面にしめ縄を張って一切の立ち入りを禁じ、氏子も鳴り物・高声を禁じられる。この神事は五穀豊穣と災難除けを祈るもので、古代の祭りの様子を伝える神事であるとして町の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 === 文化財 === === 玉村町指定重要無形民俗文化財 === * 麦蒔御神事[http://www.town.tamamura.lg.jp/rekishi_bunka/matsuri/mati-mukeiminzoku.html#mugimaki 麦蒔御神事]（玉村町ホームページ）。 - 平成12年4月27日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}大字下之宮甲524 '''周辺''' * 東林寺 - 旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}『群馬県の地名』下之宮村項。。火雷神社に隣接する。 * {{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}} （{{jalink|伊勢崎市|Q328596}}東上之宮町、{{coord|36|18|39.99|N|139|9|5.17|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|name=倭文神社（上之宮）}}） - 上之宮。利根川を挟んで対峙する。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 群馬県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}、1987年）佐波郡玉村町 下之宮村項・火雷神社項 * 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』（岩田書院、2000年）pp. 282-283 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}} === 外部リンク === {{jalink|上野国|Q1054838}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = （伝）第10代{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}元年 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 下之宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月3日|Q2509}} |神事 = 麦蒔御神事（旧暦10月末-11月初） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=火雷神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''火雷神社'''（からいじんじゃ/ほのいかづちじんじゃ「からい」：群馬県神社庁、「ほのいかづち」：『群馬県の地名』。）は、{{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}、{{jalink|上野国|Q1054838}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|利根川|Q177452}}右岸に鎮座しており、左岸に鎮座する{{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}}（上野国九宮、上之宮）に対して「'''下之宮'''（しものみや）」と称される。 === 祭神 === '''主祭神''' * '''{{jalink|火雷大神|Q10892534|火雷神}}''' （ほのいかづちのかみ）祭神の記載は、拝殿横の説明板による。 '''配祀神''' * {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323|保食命}}、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真命}}、那波八郎命 * {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}}、{{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主命}}、{{jalink|建御名方神||健御名方命}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞鳴尊}}、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高淤賀美命}} * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|大日孁貴命}}、{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝では、第10代{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}元年の創建という『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』pp. 282-283。。その後{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}（第12代）の代に東国に派遣された{{jalink|御諸別王|Q17230461}}（崇神天皇四世孫）も当社を祭祀したと伝える。当社の奉斎氏族は、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}で功を成した佐味氏（佐味君）と考えられている。 鎮座地の地名「下之宮」は、当社に由来するとされており、利根川を挟んで「上之宮」の{{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}}と対峙するが、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}の利根川変流まで両社は地続きであったという。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は、『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）に{{jalink|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|Q11634952|賀茂神社}}・{{jalink|美和神社 (桐生市)|Q11608848|美和神社}}とともに「{{jalink|那波郡|Q11642759}}火雷神」を官社と為すという記事である。これら3神は、いずれも大和地方からの勧請と見られている（賀茂 = {{jalink|高鴨神社|Q11673295}}、美和 = {{jalink|大神神社|Q705542}}、火雷 = {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}}）。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では上野国那波郡に「火雷神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。『日本後紀』『延喜式』いずれにも「那波郡」と記載されるが、この郡は{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}（明治に{{jalink|佐位郡|Q11382916}}・那波郡を合併）の前身にあたる。 {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）の『上野国交替実録帳』には「正二位火雷明神社」と記されるとともに「神館壱宇、鳥居壱基、間垣壱廻」と記載がある。また『上野国神名帳』では、総社本では鎮守10社の8番目、一宮本では鎮守12社の10番目に「従一位火雷大明神」と記載されている。 {{jalink|南北朝時代 (日本)|Q264012|南北朝時代}}成立の『{{jalink|神道集|Q2616931}}』では「八宮ヲハ那波ノ上ノ宮、火雷神ト申ス」と記載があることから、当社は上野国の{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}であったと見られている（「上ノ宮」は誤記と見られる）。また同集によると、当社の{{jalink|本地仏}}は{{jalink|虚空蔵菩薩|Q416174}}であった。中世には{{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}}・{{jalink|新田氏|Q2274150}}から崇敬され、盛時には4町四方の社域を有したが、那波氏衰退とともに当社も零落したと伝える。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}により「火雷天神社」と称された。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）7月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168}} ** {{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）8月16日、官社に列す （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』）『日本後紀』延暦15年（796年）8月16日条。 * 六国史以後 ** {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）、正二位 （『上野国交替実録帳』）『群馬県の地名』火雷神社項。 - 表記は「火雷明神社」。 ** 従一位 （『上野国神名帳』） - 表記は「火雷大明神」。 === 境内 === 本殿は三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}で、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}2年（{{jalink|1645年|Q6925}}）に造営され、以後修復を繰り返した。 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|石祠群 === 祭事 === * 例祭 （4月3日） * 小祭 （10月17日） 上記のほか、旧暦10月末の午の日午前0時から旧暦11月初午の日の夜明けにかけての13日間、特殊神事「'''麦蒔御神事'''（むぎまきごしんじ）」が行われる。神事は貞観4年（{{jalink|862年|Q24993}}）に始まったといわれる。旧暦10月末の午の日の丑刻に秘密神事を行い、燈火を用いず微声で祝詞を奏上する境内説明板。。その後は旧暦11月初午の日まで、神社四面にしめ縄を張って一切の立ち入りを禁じ、氏子も鳴り物・高声を禁じられる。この神事は五穀豊穣と災難除けを祈るもので、古代の祭りの様子を伝える神事であるとして町の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 === 文化財 === === 玉村町指定重要無形民俗文化財 === * 麦蒔御神事[http://www.town.tamamura.lg.jp/rekishi_bunka/matsuri/mati-mukeiminzoku.html#mugimaki 麦蒔御神事]（玉村町ホームページ）。 - 平成12年4月27日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}大字下之宮甲524 '''周辺''' * 東林寺 - 旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}『群馬県の地名』下之宮村項。。火雷神社に隣接する。 * {{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}} （{{jalink|伊勢崎市|Q328596}}東上之宮町、{{coord|36|18|39.99|N|139|9|5.17|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|name=倭文神社（上之宮）}}） - 上之宮。利根川を挟んで対峙する。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 群馬県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}、1987年）佐波郡玉村町 下之宮村項・火雷神社項 * 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』（岩田書院、2000年）pp. 282-283 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}} === 外部リンク === {{jalink|上野国|Q1054838}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = （伝）第10代{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}元年 |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = 下之宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月3日|Q2509}} |神事 = 麦蒔御神事（旧暦10月末-11月初） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=火雷神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''火雷神社'''（からいじんじゃ/ほのいかづちじんじゃ「からい」：群馬県神社庁、「ほのいかづち」：『群馬県の地名』。）は、{{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}、{{jalink|上野国|Q1054838}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|利根川|Q177452}}右岸に鎮座しており、左岸に鎮座する{{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}}（上野国九宮、上之宮）に対して「'''下之宮'''（しものみや）」と称される。 === 祭神 === '''主祭神''' * '''{{jalink|火雷大神|Q10892534|火雷神}}''' （ほのいかづちのかみ）祭神の記載は、拝殿横の説明板による。 '''配祀神''' * {{jalink|保食神|Q2280323|保食命}}、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005|菅原道真命}}、那波八郎命 * {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}}、{{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主命}}、{{jalink|建御名方神||健御名方命}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|誉田別命}}、{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞鳴尊}}、{{jalink|淤加美神|Q644201|高淤賀美命}} * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|宇迦之御魂命}}、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|大日孁貴命}}、{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝では、第10代{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}元年の創建という『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』pp. 282-283。。その後{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}（第12代）の代に東国に派遣された{{jalink|御諸別王|Q17230461}}（崇神天皇四世孫）も当社を祭祀したと伝える。当社の奉斎氏族は、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}で功を成した佐味氏（佐味君）と考えられている。 鎮座地の地名「下之宮」は、当社に由来するとされており、利根川を挟んで「上之宮」の{{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}}と対峙するが、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}の利根川変流まで両社は地続きであったという。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は、『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）に{{jalink|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|Q11634952|賀茂神社}}・{{jalink|美和神社 (桐生市)|Q11608848|美和神社}}とともに「{{jalink|那波郡|Q11642759}}火雷神」を官社と為すという記事である。これら3神は、いずれも大和地方からの勧請と見られている（賀茂 = {{jalink|高鴨神社|Q11673295}}、美和 = {{jalink|大神神社|Q705542}}、火雷 = {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}}）。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では上野国那波郡に「火雷神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。『日本後紀』『延喜式』いずれにも「那波郡」と記載されるが、この郡は{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}（明治に{{jalink|佐位郡|Q11382916}}・那波郡を合併）の前身にあたる。 {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）の『上野国交替実録帳』には「正二位火雷明神社」と記されるとともに「神館壱宇、鳥居壱基、間垣壱廻」と記載がある。また『上野国神名帳』では、総社本では鎮守10社の8番目、一宮本では鎮守12社の10番目に「従一位火雷大明神」と記載されている。 {{jalink|南北朝時代 (日本)|Q264012|南北朝時代}}成立の『{{jalink|神道集|Q2616931}}』では「八宮ヲハ那波ノ上ノ宮、火雷神ト申ス」と記載があることから、当社は上野国の{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}であったと見られている（「上ノ宮」は誤記と見られる）。また同集によると、当社の{{jalink|本地仏}}は{{jalink|虚空蔵菩薩|Q416174}}であった。中世には{{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}}・{{jalink|新田氏|Q2274150}}から崇敬され、盛時には4町四方の社域を有したが、那波氏衰退とともに当社も零落したと伝える。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}により「火雷天神社」と称された。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）7月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168}} ** {{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）8月16日、官社に列す （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』）『日本後紀』延暦15年（796年）8月16日条。 * 六国史以後 ** {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）、正二位 （『上野国交替実録帳』）『群馬県の地名』火雷神社項。 - 表記は「火雷明神社」。 ** 従一位 （『上野国神名帳』） - 表記は「火雷大明神」。 === 境内 === 本殿は三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}で、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}2年（{{jalink|1645年|Q6925}}）に造営され、以後修復を繰り返した。 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|石祠群 === 祭事 === * 例祭 （4月3日） * 小祭 （10月17日） 上記のほか、旧暦10月末の午の日午前0時から旧暦11月初午の日の夜明けにかけての13日間、特殊神事「'''麦蒔御神事'''（むぎまきごしんじ）」が行われる。神事は貞観4年（{{jalink|862年|Q24993}}）に始まったといわれる。旧暦10月末の午の日の丑刻に秘密神事を行い、燈火を用いず微声で祝詞を奏上する境内説明板。。その後は旧暦11月初午の日まで、神社四面にしめ縄を張って一切の立ち入りを禁じ、氏子も鳴り物・高声を禁じられる。この神事は五穀豊穣と災難除けを祈るもので、古代の祭りの様子を伝える神事であるとして町の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 === 文化財 === === 玉村町指定重要無形民俗文化財 === * 麦蒔御神事[http://www.town.tamamura.lg.jp/rekishi_bunka/matsuri/mati-mukeiminzoku.html#mugimaki 麦蒔御神事]（玉村町ホームページ）。 - 平成12年4月27日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}大字下之宮甲524 '''周辺''' * 東林寺 - 旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}『群馬県の地名』下之宮村項。。火雷神社に隣接する。 * {{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}} （{{jalink|伊勢崎市|Q328596}}東上之宮町、{{coord|36|18|39.99|N|139|9|5.17|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|name=倭文神社（上之宮）}}） - 上之宮。利根川を挟んで対峙する。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 群馬県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}、1987年）佐波郡玉村町 下之宮村項・火雷神社項 * 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』（岩田書院、2000年）pp. 282-283 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}} === 外部リンク === 創建 === 社伝では、第10代{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}元年の創建という『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』pp. 282-283。。その後{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}（第12代）の代に東国に派遣された{{jalink|御諸別王|Q17230461}}（崇神天皇四世孫）も当社を祭祀したと伝える。当社の奉斎氏族は、{{jalink|壬申の乱|Q2378252}}で功を成した佐味氏（佐味君）と考えられている。 鎮座地の地名「下之宮」は、当社に由来するとされており、利根川を挟んで「上之宮」の{{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}}と対峙するが、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}の利根川変流まで両社は地続きであったという。 === 概史 === 国史の初見は、『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）に{{jalink|賀茂神社 (桐生市)|Q11634952|賀茂神社}}・{{jalink|美和神社 (桐生市)|Q11608848|美和神社}}とともに「{{jalink|那波郡|Q11642759}}火雷神」を官社と為すという記事である。これら3神は、いずれも大和地方からの勧請と見られている（賀茂 = {{jalink|高鴨神社|Q11673295}}、美和 = {{jalink|大神神社|Q705542}}、火雷 = {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}}）。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では上野国那波郡に「火雷神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社}}に列している。『日本後紀』『延喜式』いずれにも「那波郡」と記載されるが、この郡は{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}（明治に{{jalink|佐位郡|Q11382916}}・那波郡を合併）の前身にあたる。 {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）の『上野国交替実録帳』には「正二位火雷明神社」と記されるとともに「神館壱宇、鳥居壱基、間垣壱廻」と記載がある。また『上野国神名帳』では、総社本では鎮守10社の8番目、一宮本では鎮守12社の10番目に「従一位火雷大明神」と記載されている。 {{jalink|南北朝時代 (日本)|Q264012|南北朝時代}}成立の『{{jalink|神道集|Q2616931}}』では「八宮ヲハ那波ノ上ノ宮、火雷神ト申ス」と記載があることから、当社は上野国の{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379|八宮}}であったと見られている（「上ノ宮」は誤記と見られる）。また同集によると、当社の{{jalink|本地仏}}は{{jalink|虚空蔵菩薩|Q416174}}であった。中世には{{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}}・{{jalink|新田氏|Q2274150}}から崇敬され、盛時には4町四方の社域を有したが、那波氏衰退とともに当社も零落したと伝える。 {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}により「火雷天神社」と称された。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）7月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168}} ** {{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}15年（{{jalink|796年|Q33452}}）8月16日、官社に列す （『{{jalink|日本後紀|Q1077029}}』）『日本後紀』延暦15年（796年）8月16日条。 * 六国史以後 ** {{jalink|長元|Q1193557}}3年（{{jalink|1030年|Q19301}}）、正二位 （『上野国交替実録帳』）『群馬県の地名』火雷神社項。 - 表記は「火雷明神社」。 ** 従一位 （『上野国神名帳』） - 表記は「火雷大明神」。 === 境内 === 本殿は三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}で、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}2年（{{jalink|1645年|Q6925}}）に造営され、以後修復を繰り返した。 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|石祠群 === 祭事 === * 例祭 （4月3日） * 小祭 （10月17日） 上記のほか、旧暦10月末の午の日午前0時から旧暦11月初午の日の夜明けにかけての13日間、特殊神事「'''麦蒔御神事'''（むぎまきごしんじ）」が行われる。神事は貞観4年（{{jalink|862年|Q24993}}）に始まったといわれる。旧暦10月末の午の日の丑刻に秘密神事を行い、燈火を用いず微声で祝詞を奏上する境内説明板。。その後は旧暦11月初午の日まで、神社四面にしめ縄を張って一切の立ち入りを禁じ、氏子も鳴り物・高声を禁じられる。この神事は五穀豊穣と災難除けを祈るもので、古代の祭りの様子を伝える神事であるとして町の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 === 文化財 === === 玉村町指定重要無形民俗文化財 === * 麦蒔御神事[http://www.town.tamamura.lg.jp/rekishi_bunka/matsuri/mati-mukeiminzoku.html#mugimaki 麦蒔御神事]（玉村町ホームページ）。 - 平成12年4月27日指定。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|群馬県|Q129499}}{{jalink|佐波郡|Q1075479}}{{jalink|玉村町|Q1203600}}大字下之宮甲524 '''周辺''' * 東林寺 - 旧{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}『群馬県の地名』下之宮村項。。火雷神社に隣接する。 * {{jalink|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)|Q17220856|倭文神社}} （{{jalink|伊勢崎市|Q328596}}東上之宮町、{{coord|36|18|39.99|N|139|9|5.17|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|name=倭文神社（上之宮）}}） - 上之宮。利根川を挟んで対峙する。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * 『日本歴史地名体系 群馬県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}、1987年）佐波郡玉村町 下之宮村項・火雷神社項 * 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』（岩田書院、2000年）pp. 282-283 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|那波氏|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|葛木坐火雷神社|Q11620954}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * [http://www.town.tamamura.lg.jp/site/kankou/jinja-tera.html 火雷神社]（玉村町ホームページ内の神社仏閣に関するページ） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] '群馬県佐波郡玉村町下之宮', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Sawa District|Q1075479}}{{jalink|Tamamura|Q1203600}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Kozuke Province|Q1054838}} {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379|eighth shrine}}
former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} | architecture = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{jalink|April 3|Q2509}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{jalink|Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{jalink|Nawa clan|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

'群馬県佐波郡玉村町下之宮', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Sawa District|Q1075479}}{{jalink|Tamamura|Q1203600}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Kozuke Province|Q1054838}} {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379|eighth shrine}}
former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} | architecture = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{jalink|April 3|Q2509}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{jalink|Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{jalink|Nawa clan|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} | architecture = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{jalink|April 3|Q2509}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{jalink|Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{jalink|Nawa clan|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{jalink|Nawa clan|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden | location = {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Sawa District|Q1075479}}{{jalink|Tamamura|Q1203600}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Kozuke Province|Q1054838}} {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379|eighth shrine}}
former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} | architecture = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{jalink|April 3|Q2509}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{jalink|Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{jalink|Nawa clan|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'群馬県佐波郡玉村町下之宮', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Sawa District|Q1075479}}{{jalink|Tamamura|Q1203600}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{jalink|Kozuke Province|Q1054838}} {{jalink|ichinomiya|Q1656379|eighth shrine}}
former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} | architecture = Three-bay {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{jalink|April 3|Q2509}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{jalink|Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|Karai-no-Kami}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{jalink|Nawa clan|Q11642757}} * {{jalink|Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'群馬県佐波郡玉村町下之宮', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'群馬県佐波郡玉村町下之宮', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:上野国|社8]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
