'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|WD=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|WD=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|WD=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|WD=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|WD=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]]

'群馬県佐波郡玉村町下之宮', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} [[JA:火雷神社 (玉村町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17220848}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Karai Shrine | image = [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-1.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|qid=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sawa District|Q1075479|qid=Q1075479|1=Sawa District}}{{ill|lt=Tamamura|Q1203600|qid=Q1203600|1=Tamamura}} Shimonomiya-ko 524 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|18|9.60|N|139|9|5.3|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Karai Shrine}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|qid=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kozuke Province|Q1054838|qid=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|lt=ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=eighth shrine|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | founded = (trad.) first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | architecture = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Shimonomiya | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|qid=Q2509|1=April 3}} | rituals = Mugimaki ritual (late lunar Oct to early lunar Nov) }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Karai Shrine''' (からいじんじゃ / ほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma. It is a shikinaisha and was regarded as the eighth ichinomiya of Kozuke Province. Former modern rank: gosha. Located on the right bank of the Tone River, it is called "Shimonomiya" in contrast to {{ill|lt=Shitori Shrine (Isesaki)|Q17220856|qid=Q17220856|1=Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)}} on the opposite bank, known as "Kaminomiya." === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Honoi-kazuchi no Okami|Q10892534|qid=Q10892534|lt=Karai-no-Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} ; Co-enshrined deities Includes Ukemochi, Sugawara no Michizane, Kagutsuchi, Omononushi, Takaminakata, Emperor Ojin, Susanoo, Takao-kami, Ukanomitama, Amaterasu, and Sukunahikona, among others. === History === Shrine tradition places foundation in the first year of Emperor Sujin. The place-name "Shimonomiya" is said to derive from this shrine. Tradition also says Shimonomiya and Kaminomiya were contiguous land before medieval shifts in the Tone River channel. The earliest national historical record is a 796 entry in ''Nihon Koki'' listing "Karai deity of Nawa District" as an official shrine alongside Kamo and Miwa shrines in the region. These are thought to reflect invitations of deities from Yamato-related cult centers. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' "Karai Shrine" is listed for Nawa District, Kozuke Province. In medieval records and ''Shinto-shu,'' it appears associated with Kozuke's eighth ichinomiya status. The shrine was revered by the Nawa and Nitta clans; its domain later declined with the Nawa clan. In early modern times it was also called "Karai Tenjinsha." In July 1872 it was ranked gosha. === Divine rank === * 796-08-16: entered official shrine register (''Nihon Koki'') * 1030: recorded as Sho-nii ("Karai Myojinsha") in Kozuke transfer records * Later listed as Ju-ichii in ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'' === Precinct === The honden is a three-bay nagare-zukuri building constructed in 1645 and repeatedly repaired thereafter. File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) honden.JPG|Honden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) haiden-2.JPG|Haiden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Karai-jinja (Tamamura) sekishi.JPG|Stone shrine group === Festivals === * Grand festival (April 3) * Minor festival (October 17) * Special rite "Mugimaki Goshinji" (13 days from late lunar October ox-day midnight to dawn of first horse day of lunar November) During Mugimaki, secret rites are performed at night without lamps and with low voice norito. Shimenawa are stretched around the shrine and entry is prohibited; parishioners also refrain from loud sounds. The rite prays for harvest and protection from calamity and is designated a town important intangible folk cultural property. === Cultural properties === === Tamamura Town designated important intangible folk cultural property === * Mugimaki Goshinji (designated April 27, 2000) === Site information === * Address: Shimonomiya-ko 524, Tamamura, Sawa District, Gunma * Nearby: Torinji (former betto temple), and Shitori Shrine (Kaminomiya) across Tone River === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory boards * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Gunma place names'' * ''Basic Studies on the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Nawa clan|Q11642757|qid=Q11642757|1=Nawa clan}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi-ni-imasu-Honoi-kazuchi Shrine|Q11620954|qid=Q11620954|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Karai-jinja (Tamamura)}} * Tamamura town page for Karai Shrine {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Karai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:玉村町の建築物]] [[Category:玉村町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]]
